overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a highly targeted and engaged professional presence across LinkedIn, Bluesky, and Mastodon. His content focuses on computational social science, social media analysis, and disinformation research. Across his total audience of 3,580 followers, his posts generate strong engagement, driven primarily by announcements of new research, tool releases like CooRnet v2.0, and academic hiring opportunities.

contentThemes.description: Announcements of newly published papers and updates to research software like CooRnet.
Recruitment posts for postdoctoral researchers and PhD opportunities in computational social science.
Updates regarding newly awarded research grants for studying information operations.
Insights on computational methods for detecting coordinated behavior and conference presentations.

recommendations: Leverage Bluesky's high engagement rate by posting more frequently and using it as the primary channel for rapid discussion of research findings.
Capitalize on LinkedIn's large follower base to broadcast professional milestones, funding news, and job openings, ensuring posts include direct call-to-action links.
Cross-promote software updates and tools like CooRnet on all platforms with visual media (e.g., screenshots, architecture diagrams) to drive higher click-through rates.
Standardize the use of relevant academic hashtags (such as #CompSocSci and #Disinformation) across Mastodon and LinkedIn to increase content discoverability.

platformInsights.bluesky: With an average engagement rate of 6.14%, Bluesky is the most interactive platform. It excels in community sharing, as evidenced by high repost-to-like ratios on academic papers and opportunities.
platformInsights.mastodon: An active academic hub showing moderate engagement (average 4.55%). Software releases and methodological discussions resonate well here, but follower growth could be accelerated with more consistent posting.
platformInsights.linkedin: Houses the largest audience (1,850 followers) and yields high absolute interaction numbers. Professional milestones and job postings generate significant volume of comments and likes, proving highly effective for networking.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
